DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION: FOLLOW UP WITH NO RESPONSE BY DEFENDANT. MOTION IS DENIED. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.132 TO JUDGE PRO TEM NOAH LEBOWITZ AT NOAH@NDLLEGAL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: NOAH LEBOWITZ; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1324. FROM WHAT THE COURT CAN DISCERN, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO ADD A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SEC. 6455. THAT SECTION ALLOWS A CLAIM WHERE THERE IS A DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. THE PROPOSED ALLEGATIONS AT MOST ALLEGE A THREAT TO DISCLOSE-WHICH IS NOT ACTIONABLE-RATHER THAN AN ACTUAL DISCLOSURE. THE PROPOSED COMPLAINT ALSO FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING "OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT" TO SUPPORT THE PROPOSED INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR FEES PER CCP 128.5 IS DENIED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT GRANTS SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000 TO MS. TROSCLAIR AND $500 TO MS. MENDEZ. THE COURT DOES NOT ADDRESS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO DISMISS; THE DEFENDANT MAY FILE A MOTION LATER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTIONS OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/SRB)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
Off Calendar Jan-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KELLI MENDEZ, KATHY TROSCLAIR's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7.

Defendants Kathy Lynn Trosclair and Kelli Mendez's motion for sanctions against Plaintiff Carl A. Wescott pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 is granted. No opposition was filed. Defendants served Plaintiff with the sanctions motion by mail and email on November 27, 2018, more than 21 days before filing it, as required by the safe harbor provision of section 128.7, and Plaintiff did not withdraw his complaint. (Dees Dec. 2 & Exs 1, 2.) Defendants' declarations and the Court's docket, of which the Court takes judicial notice, establish that the allegations and claims presented in the complaint lack evidentiary support (indeed, that many of the factual allegations are false and frivolous) and/or are unlikely to have evidentiary support after a reasonable opportunity for further investigation or discovery. Further, Plaintiff has been found a vexatious litigant by this Court, has repeatedly failed to serve papers in violation of the Code of Civil Procedure, the Rules of Court, and the Court's orders, and has been the subject of several sanctions awards by this Court. The only reasonable inference is that the complaint was presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay. Accordingly, the Court strikes Plaintiff's complaint and dismisses this action with prejudice, and awards Defendants $5,000 in monetary sanctions.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not reported. =(302/EPS)

Mtn To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathy Lynn Trosclair, Esq. 209-242-2050 of Trosclair Law Center appearing for herself and Defendant Mendez who is also present.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant also requests for sanctions and requests to dismiss. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT'S Motion To Amend Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion to amend complaint is denied. The motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324. From what the court can discern, it appears that plaintiff seeks to add a cause of action for violation of Business and Professions code sec. 6455. That section allows a claim where there is a disclosure of confidential information. The proposed allegations at most allege a threat to disclose-which is not actionable-rather than an actual disclosure. The proposed complaint also fails to allege facts showing "outrageous conduct" to support the proposed intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Defendants' request for fees per CCP 128.5 is denied.

In addition, the Court grants sanctions in the amount of $1,000 to Ms. Trosclair and $500 to Ms. Mendez. The Court does not address Defendant's request to dismiss; the Defendant may file a motion later.

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)
